description: Add a minimal interactive operator TUI that can list live threads and approvals, navigate pending approvals, approve the selected item, reject with a typed reason, and start through the existing CLI.

userStories.title: Expose live thread and approval queries for the TUI
userStories.description: As a TUI runtime, I want task-service list queries for threads and approvals so that the live operator screen can project repository-backed and in-memory state through a single boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: TaskService exposes listThreads() with stable ordering
TaskService exposes listApprovals() with stable ordering and current statuses
The same query behavior works in repository-backed mode
Focused service tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Drive approval actions through a testable TUI controller
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a small controller that owns selection and reject-input state so that approval actions can be exercised without wiring a full terminal into the tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Selection moves up and down within list bounds
Approving dispatches resumeApproval(...) for the selected approval
Reject mode buffers typed input and dispatches rejectApproval(...) with the typed reason
Escape exits reject-input mode without dispatching
Focused runtime tests pass

userStories.title: Render interactive approval state in the main TUI screen
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the TUI render path to show the selected approval, reject prompt state, and selected-thread events so that I can act on approvals from a single plain-text screen.
acceptanceCriteria: The selected approval is visibly marked in rendered state
Reject-input mode exposes the current prompt and buffer
The event list follows the selected approval's thread
Footer help reflects the current interaction mode
Focused TUI projection tests pass

userStories.title: Start the interactive TUI from the CLI
userStories.description: As a local operator, I want a tui command in the existing CLI so that I can launch the interactive approval console without using a separate entrypoint.
acceptanceCriteria: runCli(["tui"]) bootstraps the composed app
The tui command starts the TUI runtime
Usage text includes tui
Existing message, approve, and reject commands still work unchanged
Focused CLI tests pass

userStories.title: Verify and track the interactive TUI slice
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the TUI slice verified and reflected in Ralph tracking so that the current branch status matches the implemented feature set and verification evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Focused verification for service, tui app, tui runtime, and cli passes
Full npm test passes
Typecheck passes
The prior Ralph run is archived before reusing the active tracking files
ralph/prd.json and ralph/progress.txt describe the TUI interaction slice
